Lebanon Electoral Platform

Lebanese Parliamentary Elections Simulator

Configure district by sect, enter candidate votes, and simulate winners.

This tool simulates Lebanese parliamentary seat allocation by district using list votes, , and the district's fixed quotas. Select a district, load or enter vote data, then run the simulation to see how seats and winners change.

How to start

Pick a district, then either load a verified election baseline or create a fresh simulation.

  • Choose a district template from the selector above.
  • Load 2018 or 2022 results after selecting a district.
  • Add a new simulation if you want a blank custom scenario.

District & Candidates

Scenario Inputs

District Configuration

Template Seat Map

Start by choosing a district. The seat map, candidate builder, and simulation analytics appear after a template is loaded.

Deterministic Output

Simulation workspace is waiting for a district

Load a district template first. Metrics, winners, seat allocation tables, and visual analytics stay collapsed until the district is defined.

List EQ Allocation

EQ Status Seats

Winning Candidates

Candidate

District Visual Analytics

Seat Map Summary

List Seat Share

Seats won out of the district total.

Vote Share vs Seat Share

See where seat power runs ahead of vote weight.

Closest Races

The tightest sect contests in the current run.

Seat Gain Thresholds

How close each list is to gaining or risking a seat.

Sect Quota Bars

Stacked quota bars showing which lists currently occupy each sect seat.

List Vote Bars

Raw list votes compared with the actual list color palette.

Seat Pie Chart

3D-style view of seats won by each list.

Competitiveness Lollipops

Gap-to-challenger lines for each sect contest.

List Sect Capture

Visual map of which sect seats each list is currently taking.

Candidate Leaderboard Ribbons

Top candidates by votes, grouped by sect and colored by list.

Seat quotas are loaded from templates and locked in-app to preserve calculation integrity. Validate official seat maps before publishing final outcomes.
